titleStmt.title: SII 38.360: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 360. (A.R. No. 360 of 1912.) DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

summary.p: Badly engraved. This inscription records that Nayaṉappa Nāyakkar, an agent of the official (aḍaippa) Mallappa-Nāyakkar, assign certain persons to provide ghee for conducting rituals on the occasion of tiruvādirai to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkapuram, for the merit of Amalappa-Nāyakkar.
The temple accountant figures as signatory. It further records that this was the charity of Chellapperumāḷ piḷḷai.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
cupakiṟuti varuṣam taimātam [10 3] nāḷ śrīmat Aṭaiyappa mallappanāya
kkar Ayyaṉ kāriyattukkukaṭaivar nayiṉappanāyakkar
Amalappanāyakkar Ayyaṉukku puṇṇiyamāka tevakkāpuram tiruma
lar Uṭaiyanayiṉāṟku tiruvātiraitirunāḷukku neyi Āṭi Aru
ḷukaiyil karaiyirāyapāṭi cevvaṇman taṭi Aḷakkum cūlattāl neyikku 3 1000 kaṉṉara kaṉṉaṉ neyi kalavaippaṟavu
kāyam Eṟaṉum neyi 3 10 4 § Āka neyi �nya Inta neyi tiruvātirai tirunāḷukku Aḷakkakaṭaivar Ākavum
keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa toṣattilepoka kaṭaivar Ākavum pirāmaṇaṉaikkoṉṟa toṣat —
maikku koyilkaṇakkupiḷḷai Eḻuttu § cellapperumāḷpiḷḷai taṉmam § tiruviti cevvaṇṇa
Digital edition of SII 38.360 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
